title: Inpatient Mental Health Facilities - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Inpatient Mental Health Facilities 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON STATE HOSPITAL OREGON STATE HOSPITAL JUNCTION CITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF State-administered psychiatric facility provides inpatient psychiatric care. 29398 Recovery Way Junction City, OR 97448 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who need intensive psychiatric treatment for severe and persistent mental illness. Serves patients in the following commitment categories: Civil: People who have been found by the courts to be an imminent danger to themselves or others, or who are unable to provide for their own basic health and safety needs due to their mental illness. Includes both involuntary commitment and voluntary commitment by guardian. Also applies to people who require hospital-level neuropsychiatric care for dementia, organic brain injury, or other mental illness, often with co-occurring significant medical issues. Guilty Except for Insanity (GEI): People who committed a crime related to their mental illness. Depending on the nature of their crime, patients are under the jurisdiction of either the Psychiatric Security Review Board for more severe crimes or the Oregon State Hospital Review Panel for lesser crimes. Aid and Assist: People who have been arrested but are not able to participate in their trial because of a mental illness. Courts issue an order to send defendants to the Oregon State Hospital for stabilization and mental health treatment that enables them to understand the criminal charges against them and "aid and assist" in their own defense. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 465-2554 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE HOSPITAL OREGON STATE HOSPITAL SALEM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF State-administered psychiatric facility provides inpatient psychiatric care. 2600 Center Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who need intensive psychiatric treatment for severe and persistent mental illness. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 945-2800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITY CENTER FOR BEHAVIORAL HEALTH INPATIENT AND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides psychiatric inpatient services for youth and adults, and a Psychiatric Emergency Service (PES). On-site representatives from various community partners and peer support personnel offer transitional support services. 1225 NE 2nd Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Inpatient: Ages 9-70 PES: Age 18 and older Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 944-8000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WALLOWA VALLEY CENTER FOR WELLNESS RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residential services for individuals with severe and persistent mental illness who are also medically fragile. Services include 24/7 nursing, medication management, case management, and active therapy. 606 Medical Parkway Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday 8am-5pm, Tuesday/Wednesday 7am-7pm, Thursday 8am-7pm, Friday 8am-noon, Saturday 9am-1pm (541) 426-4524 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE HOSPITAL OREGON STATE HOSPITAL JUNCTION CITY OREGON STATE HOSPITAL OREGON STATE HOSPITAL SALEM UNITY CENTER FOR BEHAVIORAL HEALTH INPATIENT AND EMERGENCY SERVICES WALLOWA VALLEY CENTER FOR WELLNESS RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT
